document.source_title: World Urban Forum opens in Baku as housing crisis and climate shocks intensify
document.summary: The World Urban Forum, organized by UN-Habitat and Azerbaijan, opens on May 17 and runs until May 22, 2026. The forum addresses the global housing crisis, with nearly 2.8 billion people living in inadequate conditions and 300 million without homes. Discussions will focus on the impacts of climate change, informal settlements, and the need for collective action to create resilient urban environments.
key_takeaways: The forum theme is 'Housing the World: Safe and Resilient Cities and Communities'.
2.8 billion people live in inadequate housing; 300 million are homeless.
The crisis affects both the Global South and North, exacerbated by rising living costs.
Informal settlements are seen as both a challenge and an opportunity.
Climate change is a key driver of the housing crisis, with extreme weather displacing millions.
The forum marks the tenth anniversary of the New Urban Agenda.
document.why_it_matters: The World Urban Forum serves as a critical platform for global leaders to address the interconnected issues of housing, climate resilience, and urban planning, aiming to foster collaborative solutions to the escalating housing crisis.

claims.claim_text: Nearly 2.8 billion people live in inadequate housing conditions.
claims.evidence_text: According to the UN, nearly 2.8 billion people today are living in inadequate housing conditions.

claims.claim_text: The global housing crisis is being felt in both the Global South and North.
claims.evidence_text: This crisis has long been most severe and structural in the Global South, but now it is also being felt in the Global North as well.

claims.claim_text: Extreme weather events displaced more than 20 million people in 2023.
claims.evidence_text: Extreme weather events – including floods, storms and wildfires – displaced more than 20 million people in 2023 alone.

entities.name: UN-Habitat
entities.description: The United Nations agency responsible for promoting sustainable urban development.
entities.evidence_text: The conference, organized by UN-Habitat together with Azerbaijan, opens on Sunday 17 May.

entities.name: UN Development Programme (UNDP)
entities.description: A UN agency focused on global development and poverty reduction.
entities.evidence_text: Francine Pickup, Deputy Director of the UN Development Programme and head of the UNDP delegation to WUF13.

events.evidence_text: The conference, organized by UN-Habitat together with Azerbaijan, opens on Sunday 17 May and runs through Friday, 22 May.
